strategy_summary: Compounds, such as sugar, in the blood of wood frogs protect them from freezing temperatures by affecting how water freezes in the body. “The North American wood frog ( Rana sylvatica ), for instance, can survive freezing temperatures for as long as seven months, relying on a natural antifreeze in its blood to protect…
mechanism:
Compounds, such as sugar, in the blood of wood frogs protect them from freezing temperatures by affecting how water freezes in the body.

“The North American wood frog ( Rana sylvatica ), for instance, can survive freezing temperatures for as long as seven months, relying on a natural antifreeze in its blood to protect its organs.” (Morell 2001)
